phrases.intent_en: Ask how many steps there are to the top of the pagoda
phrases.text_local: Combien y a-t-il de marches jusqu'au sommet de la pagode ?
phrases.pronunciation: kom-BYAN ee ah-TEEL duh MARSH zhoos-KOH som-AY duh lah pah-GOD
phrases.cultural_note: The pagoda has 149 steps across seven narrowing storeys. Built in 1778, it is an unusual example of chinoiserie in the Loire Valley.

responses.english: About a hundred and fifty steps.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the view from the top
phrases.text_local: Peut-on voir la forêt et la Loire depuis le sommet ?
phrases.pronunciation: puh-TON vwar lah foh-RAY ay lah LWAR duh-PWEE luh som-AY
phrases.cultural_note: From the top of the pagoda you can see the surrounding Amboise forest and, on clear days, the Loire River winding through the valley.

responses.english: Yes, on a clear day you can see the Loire.
responses.phonetic: wee, par TAHN KLEHR ohn vwah lah LWAR
responses.french: Oui, par temps clair on voit la Loire.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the forest park is free to walk in
phrases.text_local: Le parc forestier est-il accessible librement ?
phrases.pronunciation: luh PARK foh-res-TYAY ay-TEEL ak-ses-EEBL lee-bruh-MAHN
phrases.cultural_note: The park around the pagoda, once part of a grand château demolished after the Revolution, is included with the pagoda entry ticket.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the park is accessible for strollers
phrases.text_local: Le parc est-il accessible avec une poussette ?
phrases.pronunciation: luh PARK eh-TEEL ak-ses-EEBL ah-VEK oon poo-SET
phrases.cultural_note: The park around the pagoda has flat paths suitable for strollers, though the pagoda steps are not accessible.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if there are boat rides on the lake
phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il des promenades en barque sur l'étang ?
phrases.pronunciation: ee ah-TEEL day proh-muh-NAD ahn BARK soor lay-TAHN
phrases.cultural_note: A small lake in front of the pagoda is a popular spot for rowboats during the summer season.
